name: Kirant Period
description: Early historical period of Nepal ruled by the Kirant dynasty. Associated with the Mahabharata era. The Kirant kings are mentioned in ancient texts and are credited with early settlement of the Kathmandu Valley.

name: Lichhavi Period
description: Golden age of Nepali art and architecture. The Lichhavi dynasty produced masterful stone sculptures, established trade with Tibet, and created the earliest surviving inscriptions in the Valley.

name: Thakuri Period
description: Transitional period between Lichhavi and Malla rule. Marked by political fragmentation but continued cultural development. The famous Nepali architect Arniko was born near the end of this era.

name: Early Malla Period
description: Period of Malla rule before the kingdom split into three. Known for codification of laws (Jayasthiti Malla Code), development of Newar arts, and construction of major temples.

name: Late Malla Period
description: Three competing Malla kingdoms (Kathmandu, Patan, Bhaktapur) drove an explosion of art, architecture, and cultural achievement. Most of the Durbar Square temples date from this era.

name: Shah Period
description: Prithvi Narayan Shah unified Nepal. The Shah dynasty expanded territory but also saw political instability, the Anglo-Nepal War (1814-16), and eventual loss of power to the Rana regime.

name: Rana Period
description: Hereditary prime ministers ruled Nepal while Shah kings were figureheads. Rana Neoclassical palaces were built, but traditional arts declined. Nepal was isolated from the outside world.

name: Modern Period
description: Democratic era beginning with the overthrow of Rana rule. Includes the Panchayat system (1960-1990), Jana Andolan movements, Maoist conflict (1996-2006), and establishment of the Federal Republic in 2008.
